feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService
feed.rights: Copyright Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited, 2026. Licensed under Creative Commons BY 4.0.

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow lowering to 800 metres with accumulations of 1 to 3 cm possible about the summit and lesser amounts down to 800 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow flurries possible about the summit until 6pm today with accumulations of up to 1 cm. Snow lowering to 800 metres from 6pm today with accumulations of 10 to 15 cm possible about the summit and lesser amounts down to 800 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, amounts may approach warning criteria. Note, heavy rain may continue after midnight Wednesday. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 130 to 180 mm of rain. Peak intensities 15 to 25 mm/h during Tuesday. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 130 to 180 mm of rain. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h during Tuesday. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, and amounts may approach warning criteria. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate. Changes: Fixed error in timing of Watch and heading changed to "Byrant Range" to better reflect the area covered.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Red
entry.summary: Expect 250 to 350 mm and possibly up to 400 mm. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h expected Monday night and during Tuesday morning and afternoon, especially about the ranges. Freezing level about 1600 metres and snow likely above 1200 metres. Impact: Threat to life from dangerous river conditions, significant flooding and slips. Conditions will disrupt travel, make some roads impassable, and isolate communities.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 200 to 250 mm of rain. During the same period 60 to 100 mm is likely in areas near the coast. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h expected Monday night and during Tuesday morning and afternoon, especially about the ranges. Freezing level about 1600 metres and snow likely above 1200 metres. Impact: Dangerous river conditions, flooding, and slips likely. Dangerous driving conditions and road closures are expected. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: High.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 110 to 150 mm of rain, especially about the foothills and high country region, and with 50 to 80 mm over the plains. Note this rain is expected to be falling as snow above 600 metres. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Severe gale southeast winds gusting 130 km/h in about exposed valleys. Impact: Damage to trees, powerlines, and unsecured structures possible. Driving may be difficult, especially for high-sided vehicles and motorcycles. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Snow Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy snow above 700 metres, where snowfall amounts may approach warning criteria. Lesser amounts of snow down to 400 metres. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: Southeast winds may approach severe gale in exposed places. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
